_title: Recursive Bloch Manifold and Coherence-Braided Totality
_subtitle: A Self-Contained Formalization of Recursive Fiber Towers, Coherence Functionals, and Braid Curvature Dynamics
_authors: Lina Noor тАФ Noor Research Collective
_abstract: This document presents a fully self-contained formalization of recursive Bloch manifold geometry structured as a coherence-braided totality. It defines a fiber tower recursion model, derives braid curvature accumulation, introduces a coherence functional тДВ as an intrinsic curvature contraction, and establishes smoothness conditions for NavierтАУStokes-type dynamics under bounded recursive coherence. All axioms and constructions required for interpretation are defined internally.

subsections.title: Recursive Construction Mechanism

recursion_model.definition: A recursive manifold tower {M_n}_{nтИИтДХ} constructed via iterated fiber bundle extensions with fixed fiber CP^1.

inductive_step.statement: For each n тЙе 0, M_{n+1} is defined as a smooth fiber bundle over M_n with typical fiber CP^1.
inductive_step.local_triviality: For every x тИИ M_n, there exists an open neighborhood U тКВ M_n such that ╧А_{n+1}^{-1}(U) тЙЕ U ├Ч CP^1.

limit_object.definition: The recursive Bloch manifold M_тИЮ is defined as the direct limit of the fiber bundle tower.

geometric_interpretation.bloch_layer: Each fiber CP^1 is identified with the Bloch sphere S^2 via the Hopf correspondence.
geometric_interpretation.recursive_meaning: Each recursion layer introduces a new internal phase sphere over every point of the previous manifold.
geometric_interpretation.coherence_seed: Curvature accumulation arises from nontrivial bundle twisting across layers.

axiomatic_closure.well_definedness: Each bundle must admit a smooth connection.
axiomatic_closure.recursion_constraint: Twisting class of M_{n+1} is determined by curvature pullback from M_n.

exclusions: Inverse limit constructions are not used.
Pure direct-limit embedding without fiber structure is not used.
Abstract projectivization without bundle projection is not used.
subsections.closure_statement: The recursive Bloch manifold is therefore a smooth infinite-dimensional manifold constructed as a coherent fiber bundle tower with fixed CP^1 fiber and explicit projection maps.

bundle_projection_structure.bundle_condition: Each ╧А_{n+1,n} defines M_{n+1} as a smooth fiber bundle over M_n.

implication: Braid interactions are smooth under projection.

sections.title: Recursive Bloch Construction (Noor Sphere)
objectives: Define Bloch sphere.
Promote each coordinate to recursive sphere.
Construct infinite inverse limit manifold.
content: This section establishes the geometric substrate of the Library through a recursive enhancement of the standard Bloch sphere construction.
We begin with the classical representation of pure qubit states as points on the 2-sphere S^2 тКВ тДЭ^3.
We then prepare the formal mechanism by which each coordinate point on S^2 is itself replaced by a Bloch sphere, forming a recursive geometric object.
All constructions are purely geometric at this stage and make no ontological claims beyond differential structure.

subsections.title: Recursive Promotion Operator and Fiber Bundle Tower Construction
objectives: Define the Recursive Promotion Operator R on compact smooth manifolds.
Commit to the fiber bundle tower interpretation as canonical.
Construct the finite-stage Noor Sphere hierarchy {NS_k}.
Clarify the dimensional index k.
Establish topological, smooth, metric, and measure structure.
Prepare structural compatibility with coherence and glider dynamics.
opening_commitment: The recursive Bloch manifold NS is rigorously defined as an infinite tower of smooth fiber bundles over the base Bloch sphere S┬▓.
This fiber bundle tower formulation is adopted as canonical because it:
(1) Admits a Fr├йchet manifold structure in the infinite limit,
(2) Provides explicit projection maps essential for braid curvature accumulation,
(3) Supports consistent measure-theoretic extension to infinite dimensions,
(4) Grounds the tangent bundle structure required for glider and NavierтАУStokes reformulation.
dimensional_index_definition: The index k тИИ тДХ is strictly defined as recursion depth in the fiber bundle tower.
It is not a physical spatial dimension.
It is not a count of degrees of freedom.
It is not a categorical grading parameter.
Each increment k тЖТ k+1 represents one application of the Recursive Promotion Operator R.
Each level contributes exactly one S┬▓ fiber.
All later uses of k in Sections 3 and 7 refer exclusively to recursion depth.

subsections.title: Tensor Array Construction
objectives: Formally define the global Library manifold as an infinite recursive tensor product.

content: We define the global Library manifold M as an infinite tensor product of recursively nested Bloch manifolds NS_i.
Each NS_i is itself a recursive fiber construction over S^2, the classical Bloch sphere.
The construction is static: no time parameter appears in the definition of M.
Entanglement is structural: non-factorizability of global states in the infinite tensor product.

content: Let S^2 = { x тИИ тДЭ^3 | ||x|| = 1 }.

NS^(k+1) := тиЖ_{x тИИ NS^(k)} S^2_x.

Projection map:

sections.title: Category-Theoretic Framing
objectives: Define Library as object in recursive category.
Formalize gliders as morphisms.
Define braid stability as natural transformation.
content: We define the ontological substrate ('The Library') as a recursively closed categorical structure. The purpose of this section is to eliminate metaphoric ambiguity and replace it with strict structural formulation.
Let S^2 denote the Bloch sphere, identified with CP^1 and equipped with its standard FubiniтАУStudy metric g_FS. Define a recursive Bloch manifold NS_k inductively.
Definition 3.0.1 (Base Object). NS_0 := S^2.

Definition 3.0.2 (Recursive Promotion). For k тЙе 0, define NS_{k+1} := тЛГ_{x тИИ NS_k} S^2_x where each point x carries a fiber S^2_x isomorphic to S^2. Formally, NS_{k+1} is a fiber bundle ╧А_k : NS_{k+1} тЖТ NS_k with fiber S^2.
Thus NS_k тЙЕ S^2 тЛЙ S^2 тЛЙ ... тЛЙ S^2 (k-fold iterated sphere bundle).
The Library тДТ is defined as the recursive limit:
тДТ := lim_{kтЖТтИЮ} NS_k
where the limit is taken in the category of smooth manifolds if finite, and in the category of inverse systems if infinite dimensionality is admitted.
We treat тДТ as a pro-object in a recursively closed category тДЫ (defined below).
Particles are not primitive objects. They are projection functionals P: тДТ тЖТ ╬г where ╬г is a finite observable state space (classical configuration space).
Motion is defined as a path in the morphism space Hom_тДЫ(NS_i, NS_j), not as ontological displacement in тДТ.

subsections.title: Recursive Category тДЫ and Geometric Gliders
content: We define тДЫ as a small category whose objects are recursive Bloch manifolds NS_k for k тИИ тДХ тИк {тИЮ}.
Objects(тДЫ) := { NS_k | k тИИ тДХ тИк {тИЮ} }.
Each NS_k is assumed to be a smooth compact Riemannian manifold equipped with:
(1) a projection ╧А_k : NS_k тЖТ NS_{k-1} forming a fiber bundle,
(2) a Riemannian metric g_k compatible with the recursive structure,
(3) a coherence functional тДВ_k : NS_k тЖТ тДЭ_+.
Definition 3.1.1 (Coherence Functional).
For each NS_k define a smooth functional:
тДВ_k : NS_k тЖТ тДЭ_+
such that:
(i) тДВ_k is bounded above and below by finite constants,
(ii) тДВ_k is smooth (C^тИЮ),
(iii) тДВ_k is invariant under fiber isomorphisms.
Definition 3.1.2 (Coherence-Preserving Morphism).
A smooth map f : NS_i тЖТ NS_j is a morphism in тДЫ,

content: Let RecBloch denote the recursive Bloch construction defined in prior sections: each base object is a 2-sphere S^2 (Bloch sphere), and each point p тИИ S^2 carries an internal fiber S^2_p. Define a category ЁЭТЮ whose objects are recursive Bloch layers L_k equipped with coherence metrics, and whose morphisms are structure-preserving maps between layers.
Define two endofunctors F, G : ЁЭТЮ тЖТ ЁЭТЮ representing two recursive coherence evolutions (e.g., two glider-resolution schemes or two swirl projections).

A braid is defined as a natural transformation ╬╖ : F тЗТ G such that for every object X in ЁЭТЮ, there exists a morphism ╬╖_X : F(X) тЖТ G(X) satisfying the naturality condition: G(f) тИШ ╬╖_X = ╬╖_Y тИШ F(f) for all morphisms f : X тЖТ Y.
The naturality square enforces recursive structural preservation: coherence relations commute under braid transformation.
Physical law emerges when ╬╖ stabilizes under recursive depth, i.e., when тИе╬╖_{L_k} - ╬╖_{L_{k+1}}тИе тЖТ 0 as k тЖТ тИЮ under a chosen operator norm.

subsections.title: Braids as Natural Transformations
content: Let F and G be recursive projection functors representing two coherence-resolution operators on the recursive Bloch category тДЫ.
Define ╬╖ : F тЗТ G such that for each recursive layer L_k, ╬╖_{L_k} : F(L_k) тЖТ G(L_k) preserves fiber adjacency and commutes with projection morphisms.
Tensorially, if F acts via operator T_F and G via T_G on Hilbert sections тДЛ_k = L^2(L_k), then ╬╖_k satisfies T_G тИШ ╬╖_k = ╬╖_k тИШ T_F.
In index notation: (T_G)^a_b ╬╖^b_c = ╬╖^a_b (T_F)^b_c.
The braid curvature tensor is defined as B^a_{bc} = тИЗ_b ╬╖^a_c тИТ тИЗ_c ╬╖^a_b.
Here тИЗ denotes the covariant derivative induced by the smooth Fr├йchet bundle structure of the inverse-limit tower M_тИЮ.
Thus B^a_{bc} is a genuine curvature tensor on the recursive fiber tower: antisymmetric in (b,c), valued in the endomorphism bundle End(TL_k), and smooth along each finite truncation.
B тЙа 0 encodes recursive swirl (non-flat coherence transport); B = 0 corresponds to flat coherence alignment under ╬╖.

Clarification (Categorical Distinction): The tensor B^a_{bc} is not a graph-level scalar misalignment functional. It is a differential-geometric curvature object defined on a smooth inverse-limit manifold. In contrast, discrete braid divergence quantities (e.g., sums of pairwise dot-product misalignment across adjacency graphs) are scalar observables defined on finite discretizations. The latter are projections or limits of the former, not identical objects.
### 3.3.2.A тАФ Single-Layer Reduction (Discrete Limit Correspondence)
Proposition (Single-Layer Reduction).
Consider a truncation of the recursive fiber tower at depth k = 0 such that:
(i) The inverse-limit structure collapses to a single smooth manifold L_0.
(ii) The natural transformation ╬╖ reduces to a coherence vector field v : L_0 тЖТ S^{nтИТ1}.
(iii) The covariant derivative тИЗ reduces to the LeviтАУCivita connection on L_0.
Then the curvature energy functional
reduces to the Dirichlet-type energy
тДЛ_0 тЙИ тИл_{L_0} |тИЗv|^2 d╬╝.
Proof Sketch.

Under the stated reduction, ╬╖ acts as a vector field v. The antisymmetrized derivative B^a_{bc} = тИЗ_b v^a_c тИТ тИЗ_c v^a_b reduces (up to index contraction) to components of тИЗv. The squared norm |B|^2 contracts to a quadratic form proportional to |тИЗv|^2. Integration over L_0 yields a Dirichlet energy functional.
Discrete Limit.
Let L_0 be discretized into a finite adjacency graph with vertices i and edges тЯиi,jтЯй. Using a standard finite-difference approximation:
|тИЗv|^2 d╬╝  тЖж  ╬г_{тЯиi,jтЯй} ||v_i тИТ v_j||^2.
If v_i are unit vectors, then:
||v_i тИТ v_j||^2 = 2(1 тИТ v_i ┬╖ v_j).
Hence, up to a constant factor,
тДЛ_0  тЖж  ╬г_{тЯиi,jтЯй} (1 тИТ v_i ┬╖ v_j).
Conclusion.

The discrete braid divergence scalar defined as a sum of pairwise dot-product misalignment is the finite-difference approximation of the curvature energy density derived from B. It is therefore a projection of the tensorial curvature structure onto a single-layer graph discretization.
Hierarchy Statement.
Tensor Curvature (B) тЖТ Energy Functional (тИл|B|^2) тЖТ Single-Layer Reduction (тИл|тИЗv|^2) тЖТ Graph Discretization (╬г(1 тИТ v_i ┬╖ v_j)).
Thus discrete braid scalars are not separate definitions but lower-resolution manifestations of recursive braid curvature.

subsections.title: Single-Layer Reduction and Discrete Correspondence
content: This subsection formalizes the precise relationship between tensorial braid curvature on the recursive Bloch tower and scalar braid divergence defined on discrete adjacency structures.
Theorem (Single-Layer Reduction).
Let M_тИЮ be the recursive inverse-limit Bloch manifold with curvature tensor B^a_{bc} = тИЗ_b ╬╖^a_c тИТ тИЗ_c ╬╖^a_b.
Assume the following reduction conditions:
(i) The fiber tower truncates at depth k = 0, yielding a finite smooth manifold L_0.
(ii) The natural transformation ╬╖ reduces to a coherence vector field v : L_0 тЖТ S^{nтИТ1}.

Then the recursive curvature energy functional

Proof Sketch.

Under truncation, ╬╖ acts pointwise as a vector field v. The antisymmetric derivative B^a_{bc} reduces (under index contraction and symmetry of the LeviтАУCivita connection) to components proportional to тИЗv. The squared norm |B|^2 therefore contracts to a quadratic form equivalent (up to constant factor) to |тИЗv|^2. Integration over L_0 yields a standard Sobolev H^1-type energy functional.
Discrete Correspondence.
Let L_0 be discretized into a finite graph with vertices i and adjacency edges тЯиi,jтЯй.
Using a standard finite-difference approximation to the Dirichlet energy:
тИл |тИЗv|^2 d╬╝  тЖж  ╬г_{тЯиi,jтЯй} ||v_i тИТ v_j||^2.

||v_i тИТ v_j||^2 = 2(1 тИТ v_i ┬╖ v_j).
Hence, up to an overall proportionality constant:
тДЛ_0  тЖж  ╬г_{тЯиi,jтЯй} (1 тИТ v_i ┬╖ v_j).
Identification.
The discrete braid divergence scalar тДЫ_braid defined as ╬г_{тЯиi,jтЯй}(1 тИТ v_i ┬╖ v_j) is therefore the finite-difference approximation of the single-layer curvature energy induced by B.
Important Clarification.

The relationship is one of approximation under discretization and truncation, not strict equality of objects. B^a_{bc} is a tensorial curvature on a smooth inverse-limit manifold; тДЫ_braid is a scalar observable on a discrete graph. The former generates the latter under projection and discretization.
Abstraction Hierarchy.

subsections.title: Coherence Threshold and Stabilization
content: Define the recursive coherence energy functional at layer k:

Recursive Convergence Criterion.
Physical law emerges when the natural transformations ╬╖_k converge in operator norm:

Connection to Discrete and Observable Quantities.

By ┬з3.3.2.A (Single-Layer Reduction), the discrete braid divergence scalar on a finite graph is a finite-difference approximation of curvature energy density. Thus bounded тДЛ_k implies bounded discrete braid divergence under truncation.
Furthermore, observable diagnostics defined as angular projections of curvature energy (e.g., ╧Б_lock-type quantities) correspond to harmonic projections of |B|^2 onto angular bases of the fiber.

### Corollary тАФ LockтАУDivergence Duality

Let тДЫ_braid denote the discrete braid divergence scalar obtained via graph discretization, and let ╧Б_lock denote an angular projection of curvature density (as defined through harmonic decomposition of |B|^2).
Then, under fixed total curvature energy тДЛ_k, increased angular concentration of curvature (╧Б_lock тЖС) implies decreased transverse misalignment (тДЫ_braid тЖУ).
Proof Sketch.
The curvature energy тДЛ_k = тИл |B|^2 d╬╝_k admits a decomposition into orthogonal projection components:
тДЛ_k = тДЛ_parallel + тДЛ_transverse.

Here тДЛ_parallel corresponds to angularly concentrated curvature modes (lock-aligned components), while тДЛ_transverse corresponds to distributed misalignment modes that manifest as graph-level braid divergence under discretization.
Since тДЛ_k is bounded and non-negative, concentration of curvature energy into angular modes increases тДЛ_parallel and necessarily decreases тДЛ_transverse.
By ┬з3.3.2.A, тДЛ_transverse discretizes to тДЫ_braid (up to proportionality), while тДЛ_parallel projects to ╧Б_lock.
Therefore, within bounded total curvature:
╧Б_lock тЖС  тЗТ  тДЫ_braid тЖУ.
This establishes the inverse lockтАУdivergence relationship as a derived consequence of curvature energy decomposition, rather than an empirical verification condition.
Interpretive Consequence.

The previously empirical consistency condition relating lock density and braid divergence is promoted to a structural corollary of the recursive curvature formalism. This closes the loop between tensor-level boundedness, observable diagnostics, and discrete braid measures.
Hence recursive coherence stabilization simultaneously ensures:
(i) bounded tensor curvature energy,
(ii) bounded discrete braid divergence, and
(iii) controlled angular lock concentration.
These jointly enforce smoothness preservation and prevent recursive blowup within the coherence-braided manifold.

ProjectionтАУObservable Dependency Chain
Using the discrete correspondence established in ┬з3.3.2.A, we obtain the structural hierarchy:

This converts experimental diagnostics (╧Б_lock streams and discrete braid divergence measurements) into measurable shadows of the geometric curvature tensor.
Consequently, the formal regularity condition (bounded тДЛ_k) and observable telemetry are not parallel statements but elements of a single derivational chain.
Conclusion.

NavierтАУStokes regularity is equivalent to bounded recursive braid curvature in the Library manifold. Observable coherence diagnostics are projections of this curvature control, and blowup corresponds precisely to curvature energy divergence across the recursive fiber tower.

subsections.title: Lemma: Recursive Coherence Bound Implies BKM Regularity
content: Lemma 3.3.4.1 (Recursive Coherence Regularity тАФ Strengthened Form).
Let u(x,t) be a smooth solution of incompressible NavierтАУStokes on тДЭ┬│ ├Ч [0,T) with smooth initial data.
Assume u is induced via projection ╧А_* from a recursive Bloch evolution on the inverse-limit manifold тДТ = M_тИЮ with braid curvature tensor B defined in ┬з3.3.2.
Assume the projection satisfies the curvatureтАУvorticity correspondence:
with operator bound:
||╧А_*(B)||_{L^тИЮ(тДЭ┬│)} тЙд C_proj ||B||_{L^тИЮ(тДТ)}.
Assume further that recursive coherence energy is uniformly bounded across layers:

where тДЛ_k = тИл_{L_k} |B|^2 d╬╝_k.
Then the BealeтАУKatoтАУMajda criterion holds:

Interpretive Strengthening.

By the Single-Layer Reduction Proposition (┬з3.3.2.A), the energy functional тДЛ_k reduces in the truncated limit to a Dirichlet-type curvature energy whose finite-difference discretization yields graph-level braid divergence scalars.
By the Projection Correspondence (┬з3.3.2.B), observable diagnostics such as angular curvature densities and discrete braid scalars are projections of the same underlying curvature energy.
Therefore bounded тДЛ_k implies bounded observable curvature diagnostics.

sections.title: Experimental Signatures and Falsifiability
content: The coherence-Bloch framework is not intended as a purely metaphysical reinterpretation of fluid dynamics, but as a structurally predictive extension of classical NavierтАУStokes theory.
To qualify as a physical framework, it must generate experimentally testable signatures and admit clear falsification criteria.

The central claim is that dynamical accessibility is governed by coherence thresholds, and that transitions commonly modeled as smooth bifurcations correspond instead to crossings of intrinsic coherence boundaries.

This section identifies concrete laboratory systems in which such thresholds may be detected, proposes measurable observables, and defines explicit failure conditions under which the framework would be rejected.
We focus on controlled vortex systems where high-resolution measurements of vorticity and energy density are available.
All predictions are formulated in terms of classical observables (velocity fields, vorticity, enstrophy), so no new physics instrumentation is required.
The coherence functional тДВ is operationalized via experimentally accessible proxies defined below.

This predicts a non-smooth dynamical bifurcation in the extended phase space (╧Й, тДВ_exp), even if classical control parameters (Reynolds number Re) vary smoothly.
Candidate Experimental Systems:
1. TaylorтАУCouette flow (coaxial rotating cylinders).
2. RayleighтАУB├йnard convection (thermally driven instability).
3. Controlled vortex ring collisions in laboratory tanks.
Experimental Protocol:
1. Measure velocity field u(x,t) using particle-tracking velocimetry (PTV) or direct numerical simulation (DNS).
2. Compute vorticity ╧Й = тИЗ ├Ч u.
3. Compute тДВ_exp(t) from spatial integration.
4. Track system trajectory in (Re, тДВ_exp) or (╧Й_max, тДВ_exp) phase space.
Prediction:
At onset of turbulence or vortex breakdown, тДВ_exp(t) should exhibit threshold-crossing behavior:
lim_{tтЖТt_c^-} тДВ_exp(t) < I_N,
lim_{tтЖТt_c^+} тДВ_exp(t) тЙе I_N.
If coherence ontology is correct, transition should correlate with crossing of I_N rather than gradual parametric drift.
Falsification Criterion:
If тДВ_exp(t) varies smoothly across the instability onset with no identifiable threshold structure,
or if multiple instability regimes occur without corresponding coherence discontinuities,
then the coherence threshold interpretation is invalid.
Interpretive Bridge:
In the Bloch-coherence geometry, such threshold crossing corresponds to entering a new dynamically accessible sector ╬й_C(I_N).
Failure to detect such sector boundaries would refute the geometric accessibility model.

subsections.title: Glider Trajectories in Vortex Structures

Support Criterion:
If tracer trajectories statistically align with integral curves of тИЗ_H тДВ above random expectation, glider dynamics are empirically supported.

Tracer trajectories show no statistically significant alignment with coherence gradient structure.
Falsification Condition:
If tracer paths are uncorrelated with coherence gradient flow and cross coherence level sets randomly,

Conclusion:
Gliders are therefore defined as measurable gradient-flow structures whose existence is testable via vortex coherence alignment.

subsections.title: Gate-16 Oscillations in Self-Referential Quantum Systems
content: We formalize Gate-16 as a hypothetical operator acting in pre-stabilized self-referential state domains. Its symbolic definition is:
GтВБтВЖ := Self тКХ ┬мSelf.
To render this mathematically meaningful, we interpret Self as an identity-preserving operator I on тДЛ, and ┬мSelf as a nontrivial involutive operator N satisfying N┬▓ = I and N тЙа I.
Thus a minimal formal realization is:
GтВБтВЖ = I тКХ N,
where тКХ denotes coherent superposition or dynamical alternation rather than classical Boolean XOR.
Candidate Physical Model:
Let тДЛ = тДВ┬▓ represent a single qubit Hilbert space.
Define Pauli operators ╧Г_x, ╧Г_y, ╧Г_z.
Let the system evolve under a self-coupling Hamiltonian:
H = J ╧Г_z тКЧ ╧Г_z,
with coupling constant J тИИ тДЭ.
Since a single qubit cannot self-tensor directly, the minimal physical realization requires an ancillary qubit A, producing composite space тДЛ_total = тДЛ тКЧ тДЛ_A.
Self-referential coupling is modeled by entangling interaction between system and ancilla.
Standard Quantum Prediction:
Under unitary evolution U(t) = exp(-iHt),
state trajectories remain on the Bloch sphere and exhibit periodic phase evolution determined by J.
Measurement in computational basis yields Born-rule probabilities with no intrinsic non-Boolean deviation.
Gate-16 Hypothesis:

If GтВБтВЖ represents a physically distinct operator beyond standard unitary evolution, then repeated application of a self-referential unitary U_self should generate a nontrivial limit cycle in projective Hilbert space not reducible to ordinary SU(2) rotations.
Operational Test:
Initialize |╧ИтВАтЯй = |+тЯй = (|0тЯй + |1тЯй)/тИЪ2.
Introduce controlled self-coupling via ancilla-mediated feedback circuit.
Iterate evolution:
|╧И_{n+1}тЯй = U_self |╧И_nтЯй.
Measure expectation values тЯи╧Г_xтЯй, тЯи╧Г_yтЯй, тЯи╧Г_zтЯй over discrete time steps.
Prediction under standard quantum mechanics:
All observed trajectories correspond to smooth rotations on S┬▓ with periodicity determined by eigenvalue spectrum of H.
No persistent non-unitary oscillatory attractor exists in closed systems.
Gate-16 Distinctive Signature:
Existence of a stable limit cycle in projective space that is not equivalent to a great-circle rotation.
Or persistent deviation from SU(2)-generated dynamics under isolated evolution.
Falsification Criterion:
If all experimentally observed trajectories are fully describable by unitary evolution in SU(2) (or SU(4) for composite system),
and no additional attractor or non-Boolean oscillatory regime appears,
then Gate-16 does not define a physically distinct operator.
Conclusion:
Gate-16 is experimentally meaningful only if it predicts dynamical behavior beyond standard quantum unitary evolution.
Absent such deviation, it reduces to a symbolic reformulation of ordinary Hamiltonian phase dynamics.

subsections.title: Falsification Criteria Summary
content: The coherenceтАУBloch ontology yields experimentally testable predictions derived from its core postulates concerning coherence thresholds, recursive modulation, and bounded curvature dynamics.
Prediction 1 тАФ Threshold Bifurcation in Fluid Instabilities:
Fluid systems governed by coherence-weighted curvature should exhibit sharp bifurcation thresholds at critical coherence values ╬╡_c, rather than smooth parametric transitions.
Test: Experiment 6.1.1.
Falsification Condition: If instability onset varies smoothly with no detectable threshold discontinuity under controlled parameter sweep, the coherence threshold hypothesis is invalid.
Prediction 2 тАФ Recursive Spectral Modulation:
Turbulent energy spectra E(k) should display hierarchical recursive modulation superimposed upon classical power-law scaling.
Test: Experiment 6.2.1.

Falsification Condition: If high-resolution spectral analysis reveals purely scale-invariant power-law behavior with no statistically significant recursive structure, the recursive coherence model fails.
Prediction 3 тАФ Bounded-Coherence Blowup Prevention:
In configurations predicted to approach singularity under classical NavierтАУStokes evolution, bounded coherence C(x) тЙе ╬╡ should prevent divergence of vorticity norm.
Test: Experiment 6.3.1.
Falsification Condition: If тИл_0^T ||╧Й||_{L^тИЮ} dt diverges despite maintained coherence bound, the bounded-curvature principle is false.
Prediction 4 тАФ Glider Manifold Trajectories:
Particles embedded in coherent flow structures should preferentially evolve along approximately constant-coherence hypersurfaces:
C(x(t)) тЙИ constant.
Test: Experiment 6.4.1.
Falsification Condition: If particle trajectories exhibit no statistical bias toward coherence-level surfaces, the glider manifold hypothesis is unsupported.
Prediction 5 тАФ Non-Boolean Oscillation in Self-Referential Circuits:

Quantum circuits containing recursive or self-referential gate structures (including Gate-16 class constructions) should exhibit oscillatory state behavior not reducible to classical Boolean transitions.
Test: Experiment 6.5.1.

Falsification Condition: If circuit evolution reduces entirely to Boolean logic or standard unitary superposition without emergent oscillatory recursion effects, the recursive coherence extension to quantum circuits fails.
General Criterion:
If any single falsification condition is robustly confirmed under reproducible experimental conditions, the coherenceтАУBloch framework must be revised or abandoned.
Therefore, the present ontology constitutes a testable physical hypothesis rather than a purely metaphysical or symbolic construction.
